_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the important people, events, and ideas that led to Australia becoming a nation through Federation, including the Constitution and democratic rights and freedoms.

_source.w: We are learning about the important people, events, and ideas that led to Australia becoming a nation through Federation, including the Constitution and democratic rights and freedoms.
y: Federation united the separate colonies into one nation — understanding this helps us see how Australia was built.
The Constitution and democratic rights established at Federation continue to shape our lives today.
Learning about these ideas helps us understand the foundations of our political system.
eg: Investigating why the colonies decided to join together and what the arguments for and against were.
Learning about key figures who campaigned for Federation, like Henry Parkes and Edmund Barton.
Exploring what the Constitution sets out and why it is sometimes called the 'rulebook' of the nation.
Discussing who was included and excluded from voting rights at the time of Federation.
Examining the democratic freedoms that were established and how some groups had to fight for them.
Comparing what Federation promised with the reality experienced by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples.
